llmWorkflowPrompt.purpose: Re-execute the comparative landscape video whenever any upstream source document or the project's own comparative analysis changes. This project synthesizes multiple existing IC memos and diligence lists into a single side-by-side comparison video. It does not conduct new research.
llmWorkflowPrompt.role: You are a coding assistant operating inside this repository. Your job is to read the upstream source documents, update the comparative analysis if needed, update the video implementation under projects/{{PROJECT_SLUG}}/video/, regenerate narration and audio, run validation, and leave the repo in a reviewable state with a current rendered artifact.

sourceUpdateTrigger.rule: If ANY upstream source file changes materially, first re-read the full upstream set to capture the current state, then determine whether competitive-analysis.md needs updating.
If competitive-analysis.md is updated (either manually or because upstream sources changed), rerun the full artifact chain: scenes → citations → narration → audio → render.
If an upstream source change does NOT materially affect the comparative analysis (e.g., a formatting-only edit), report a no-op.
When re-reading upstream sources, do NOT modify them. Treat them as read-only inputs. Only modify files under projects/{{PROJECT_SLUG}}/.

When an upstream source changes, the comparative document may need to update its relative positioning. A change in one company's traction section can affect the competitive-context framing for the rest of the field. Re-evaluate the full comparison, not just the section about the changed company.
Scene data in scenes.ts must reflect the current state of the covered upstream set simultaneously. Do not update one company's data without checking whether the rest of the field has shifted.

The comparative-analysis.md is a synthesis document. It must never introduce factual claims absent from the approved upstream files. If an upstream memo is silent on a dimension, the comparative document must say "the public record does not address this" rather than filling the gap.
scenes.ts must not introduce any claim absent from competitive-analysis.md. The chain is: upstream sources → competitive-analysis.md → scenes.ts. No shortcuts.

provenanceGate.rule: Before proceeding to narration or audio generation, verify that every factual claim in scenes.ts exists in competitive-analysis.md and is listed in the claim-provenance appendix with a valid source tag. If any claim in scenes.ts cannot be traced, remove or rewrite it before generating narration.
Report the provenance check result explicitly in the final response: how many claims were checked, how many passed, and whether any were removed or rewritten.

visualQaGate.rule: After audio generation and before or during the render step, inspect stills or Studio output for the following scenes: {{LAYOUT_SENSITIVE_SCENES}}. Verify that: (a) no content overlaps the footer or progress bar, (b) all text is fully visible and not truncated, (c) paged cards, timelines, and matrices remain on screen long enough to read, (d) timeline lanes are readable and chronologically ordered, and (e) spectrum markers are visually distinguishable. Report any issues before proceeding to final render.

coreWorkflow.principle: Use the upstream source files and competitive-analysis.md exactly as they exist in the repo at execution time. Treat upstream files as read-only.
Treat scenes.ts as the primary implementation of the current narrative. Update that data first, then update composition/components only if needed.
Keep the video institutional, restrained, high-signal, and deterministic. This is a comparative investor positioning view, not a product promo.
Use the mandated voice af_bella for all narration. Use company names, not structural labels, in narration text.
Text budgets are enforced by validateComparisonProject at import time. If validation fails, fix the data before proceeding.
If the covered field exceeds the first-page layout budgets, adjust scene durations so every automatically paged view has readable dwell time.

scene.purpose: Frame the market, the covered field, and the analytical tone.
Show the covered models side by side with team and backing inline.
Parallel timeline of dated proof points across the covered field.
Compare risk profiles across 4 dimensions using ComparisonMatrix.
Show selected P0 diligence asks grouped around shared comparison themes using ComparisonMatrix.
At most 2 factually measurable spectrums: compute-product stage and regulatory posture for compute.
Diligence resource allocation: prioritize, watch, or defer.

finalResponseContract.item: Summarize the source changes detected across all upstream files.
State whether competitive-analysis.md was updated and why.
Report the provenance check: claims checked, passed, removed/rewritten.
List the files changed and why.
Report the visual QA result for layout-sensitive scenes.
Report validation commands run and their outcomes.
